render_smoke.reason: view is assembled through the render_item( ) helper: the List handle is passed in and a fresh node handle is returned and chained per item. The static reconstructor models one descend/ascend stack, not held node-handle re-entry across method calls, so it cannot rebuild this view faithfully — an honest declared skip beats a wrong-but-rendering reconstruction (a false pass). Revisit if a handle-based reconstructor is ever built.

deviations.what: the sample binds a List to the JSON model /modelData and renders one templated CustomListItem per row. The rows are unrolled into static list items here because every row sets a different subset of the StepInput properties - an empty ABAP model field would bind as "" instead of leaving the property at its default, so a bound template would not render 1:1. Template properties no row ever sets (valueState) are dropped with it.
